i: ZEMA — mazingira management agency. Copperbelt communities have suffered decades of SO₂ and heavy metal pollution — documented by multiple studies and legal cases (e.g., Vedanta/KCM litigation in UK courts).

First Quantum Minerals — operates Kansanshi (one of Africa's largest copper mines) and Sentinel mines. Barrick Gold — operates Lumwana copper mine. Mopani Copper Mines — majority owned by ZCCM-IH after Glencore divestment. KCM (Konkola Copper Mines) — under provisional liquidation.
Ministry of Mines and Minerals Tengenezament. ZEITI (Zambia Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — EITI member. Mining Cadastre Department — licensing authority.

National Assembly of Zambia — unicameral, 156 elected members + 8 nominated. Contact through parliament.gov.zm. Zambia is a presidential republic with competitive elections (peaceful transitions in 1991, 2011, 2021). SADC (Southern African Tengenezament Community) member.

ZW.s: Southern African uchumi ($41.5B Pato la Taifa). Mining 4.24% Pato la Taifa (platinum group metals, gold, lithium, diamonds). Kilimo 8.7%. Uzalishaji wa viwanda 15.6%. Mapato ya misitu 1.82%. Governance concerns. Major lithium reserves (Bikita, Arcadia).

sectors.w: Zimbabwe's uchimbaji madini sector is expanding rapidly (lithium — critical for EV batteries — and PGMs). Wakati kwa kila mgodi mazingira data is published alongside revenue, W inakuwa inayoweza kuhesabika.
bp: International supply chain due diligence for critical minerals (lithium for EU Battery Regulation compliance). Wakati EU buyers require mazingira verification, maslahi yanayoingiliana katika unregulated extraction inadhoofika.
Per-mine mazingira monitoring through ZEITI (Zimbabwe applied for EITI candidacy). Inafanya W iweze kuhesabika.
Inaweza kuvunjwa kwa sehemu. Lithium uchimbaji madini (hard-rock) generates waste rock and uses water. Best-practice processing and water recycling reduce utegemezi wa mfumo.
a: Ripoti kwa EMA (Environmental Management Agency)
Andika kupitia ZELA (Zimbabwe Environmental Law Association)
Report through international uchimbaji madini company compliance channels
Zimplats (Impala Platinum): Tekeleza international mazingira standards
Prospect Lithium (Bikita Minerals): Pitia responsible lithium uchimbaji madini practices
Zim Integrated (Arcadia): Apply EU Battery Regulation-aligned mazingira standards
Ministry of Mines: Tekeleza mazingira conditions in uchimbaji madini licenses
EMA: Imarisha mazingira impact assessment for lithium projects
ZMDC (Zimbabwe Mining Tengenezament Corporation): Require mazingira compliance from joint venture partners
Engage through available ya kibunge channels — Parliament of Zimbabwe: National Assembly (270 members) and Senate (80 members); note limited political pluralism in practice
Support EU Battery Regulation supply chain due diligence requirements for Zimbabwean lithium
Tetea kupitia SADC Mining Protocol for regional mazingira standards
Shinikiza kwa EITI membership to improve uchimbaji madini uwazi
i: EMA — mazingira management agency. ZELA — mazingira law organization. Note: asasi za kiraia space is constrained in Zimbabwe.

Zimplats — Zimbabwe's largest PGM producer (Impala Platinum subsidiary). Bikita Minerals — lithium uchimbaji madini (Sinomine Resources acquired). Arcadia Lithium — major lithium deposit (Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt acquired). Caledonia Mining — gold.
Ministry of Mines and Mining Tengenezament. EMA — mazingira regulation. ZMDC — state uchimbaji madini company. Zimbabwe's Mines and Minerals Act governs the sector.

Parliament of Zimbabwe — bicameral: National Assembly (270 members) and Senate (80 members). Note: ZANU-PF dominance limits effective legislative oversight. International channels: SADC, AU, EITI (Zimbabwe has expressed interest in joining).

CN.s: World's second-largest uchumi ($18,743.8B Pato la Taifa). Uzalishaji wa viwanda 24.9% Pato la Taifa — global factory floor. Mapato ya makaa ya mawe 0.61%. Viwanda 36.5%. Huduma 56.7%. One-party state (CPC). World's largest CO₂ emitter. Rapid renewable deployment alongside continued coal expansion.

sectors.w: China operates ~1,100 GW of coal capacity — more than the rest of the world combined. However, China also deploys more nishati mbadala annually than any other country. Wakati renewable deployment makes new coal uneconomic, maslahi yanayoingiliana katika coal expansion breaks.
bp: Renewable energy cost competitiveness — China's solar and wind are already cheaper than new coal in most provinces. Wakati existing coal plants become more expensive to run than new renewables, the maslahi yanayoingiliana breaks. This is already happening in eastern provinces.
Per-plant uzalishaji wa hewa data. China's national ETS (launched 2021) covers ~4.5 billion tonnes CO₂. Expanding coverage from electricity to industry sectors makes W computable.
Haiwezi kuvunjwa. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, PM2.5, and mercury by chemistry.
a: Ripoti kwa MEE (Ministry of Ecology and Environment, 生态环境部)
Report through provincial mazingira protection bureaus
Andika kupitia 12369 mazingira complaint hotline
State energy companies (CHN Energy, Huaneng, Datang, Huadian, SPIC): Accelerate coal-to-renewables transition
Reduce new coal plant approvals
Wekeza katika grid-scale storage to enable renewable integration
NEA (National Energy Administration, 国家能源局): Limit new coal approvals to replace-and-retire
MEE: Accelerate national ETS coverage expansion to cement, steel, aluminum
NDRC: Align electricity market reform with carbon reduction targets
Support US-China climate cooperation mechanisms (Sunnylands process)
Tetea kupitia COP process for China's coal phase-down commitments
Shinikiza kwa national ETS carbon price to rise above marginal abatement cost
Support technology cooperation on CCS and grid-scale storage
i: MEE (Ministry of Ecology and Environment) — central mamlaka ya mazingira. 12369 hotline — mazingira complaint system receiving millions of reports annually. Provincial Environmental Protection Bureaus — local enforcement. IPE (Institute of Public & Environmental Affairs, 公众环境研究中心) — Ma Jun's NGO providing corporate mazingira data.

China's 'Big Five' power generators: CHN Energy (国家能源集团), China Huaneng (华能), China Datang (大唐), China Huadian (华电), SPIC (国家电投). All inayomilikiwa na serikali, collectively operating most coal capacity. Also rapidly scaling renewables.

NEA (National Energy Administration) — energy planning and regulation. NDRC (National Tengenezament and Rekebisha Commission, 国家发展和改革委员会) — economic planning, sets electricity prices. National ETS — world's largest carbon market by coverage (power sector).

National People's Congress (全国人民代表大会) — ~3,000 members. China is a nchi ya chama kimoja — the NPC does not function as an independent legislature. International channels: US-China climate bilateral, COP/UNFCCC, G20. China's dual carbon targets: peak CO₂ before 2030, carbon neutrality by 2060.

KR.s: Advanced uchumi ($1,875.4B Pato la Taifa). Uzalishaji wa viwanda 26.6% Pato la Taifa (semiconductors, automobiles, shipbuilding, petrochemicals). Huduma 57.5%. Heavy energy import dependence. Fourth-largest coal importer globally. G20 member. Vibrant demokrasia.

sectors.w: South Korea operates ~37 GW of coal capacity and is the world's 4th-largest coal importer. The 10th Basic Plan for Electricity Supply and Demand targets coal phase-down. Wakati carbon pricing (K-ETS) makes coal uncompetitive versus LNG and renewables, the maslahi yanayoingiliana breaks.
bp: K-ETS (Korea Emissions Trading Scheme) carbon price escalation. Wakati carbon costs make coal-fired generation more expensive than LNG and renewables, maslahi yanayoingiliana katika coal dependence breaks.
Per-plant uzalishaji wa hewa and health cost data. Korea Environment Institute and Korean medical associations have documented coal plant health impacts. Inafanya W iweze kuhesabika.
Haiwezi kuvunjwa. Coal combustion releases SO₂, PM2.5, and CO₂.
a: Ripoti kwa Ministry of Environment (환경부)
Andika kupitia KFEM (Korean Federation for Environmental Movements, 환경운동연합)
Report through KEPCO compliance channels
KEPCO (Korea Electric Power Corporation, 한국전력공사): Tekeleza coal retirement schedule aligned with NDC
POSCO: Scale hydrogen-based steelmaking (HyREX)
Samsung, SK, Hyundai: Expand renewable electricity procurement (RE100 commitments)
MOTIE (Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy, 산업통상자원부): Accelerate coal retirement in Basic Electricity Plan
Ministry of Environment: Imarisha K-ETS carbon price floor
KPX (Korea Power Exchange): Rekebisha merit order to disadvantage coal
Wasiliana na mwakilishi wako 국회의원 (Member of the National Assembly, 300 members) kudai coal phase-out timeline in energy legislation
Support Powering Past Coal Alliance membership
Tetea kupitia OECD for coal finance phase-out
Shinikiza kwa G20 commitment to end international coal financing (South Korea pledged to end overseas coal finance in 2021)
i: Ministry of Environment (환경부) — mamlaka ya mazingira. KFEM (환경운동연합) — South Korea's largest mazingira NGO. Solutions for Our Climate (기후솔루션) — climate policy advocacy organization.
KEPCO — state electricity company (subsidiary gencos operate coal plants). POSCO — steelmaker, major coal consumer. Samsung, SK Group, Hyundai — major conglomerates with growing RE100 commitments.
MOTIE — energy and industry ministry. K-ETS — Korea Emissions Trading Scheme (launched 2015, covers ~70% of national uzalishaji wa hewa). KPX (한국전력거래소) — electricity market operator.

국회 (National Assembly) — unicameral, 300 members. Contact through assembly.go.kr. South Korea is a presidential republic with competitive elections. G20 member, OECD member, Powering Past Coal Alliance member (joined 2021).
